Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 009. Ullusunu on His Guard (CT 53 589)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

[x URU].⸢ar⸣-pad-du [x x x x x x]

(1) ... the city of A]rpad [......].

o 2'2'

⸢ú⸣-ma-a mul-lu-su-[nu x x x x x]

(2) Now, [let] Ullusunu [be] on guard for hi[mself ...]; and the letter [......] which I sent to you [......] within [......

o 3'3'

[a]-⸢na⸣ EN.NUN ša ra-⸢me⸣-[ni-šú x x x]

o 4'4'

[x x]+⸢x⸣ ù e-gír-tú ⸢x⸣+[x x x x x x]

o 5'5'

[x x] ša áš-pu-rak-kan-⸢ni⸣ [x x x x x]

o 6'6'

[x x] ina ŠÀ-⸢bi⸣ [x x x x x]

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[x x x] ⸢ni mu⸣ [x x x x]

r 2'2'

[mu]-uk mul-⸢lu⸣-[su-nu x x]

(r 2) I said: "Ull[usunu ......

r 3'3'

[x x] ⸢x⸣ [x x x x x x]

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P314001/.
